denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/04/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102945868          Group #: GRP-1372
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-154985
Claim #: CLM-2026-207717
Date of Service (requested): 01/15/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 01/17/2026 for MRI lumbar spine without contrast related to Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI lumbar spine without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM112 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
The requested service is not medically necessary because it does not meet the plan's medical necessity criteria.
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM112 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not meet medical necessity criteria because the service is not medically necessary for this member at this time. A less costly modality is appropriate before MRI lumbar spine without contrast for Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). Plan policy requires step-down diagnostic or therapeutic approach.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with modality substitution. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
If required information is not received within the applicable timeframe, the appeal may be administratively closed as withdrawn.
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102945868) and reference PA-2026-154985. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Lumbar disc herniation, suspected (M51.26). He has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI lumbar spine without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. Radicular pain 6 weeks; failed PT; straight-leg raise positive. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
